headline: Government reporting increased sharply while international logistics expanded: 1,719 deaths, 5,034 injuries, 65 provisional camps and more than 2,200 international rescuers. PAHO-identified EMT and WASH gaps remain, but incoming capacity requires location-specific gap validation.

metrics.label: REPORTED DEATHS
metrics.note: Latest government update; independently corroborated at threshold by AP.

metrics.label: REPORTED INJURIES
metrics.note: Latest government update; independent reconciliation pending.

metrics.label: AFFECTED / DISPLACED
metrics.note: Government term: damnificados; displacement status not fully reconciled.

metrics.label: INTL. RESCUE
metrics.note: 27 countries and 140 search dogs reported by OCHA.

accessStatus.detail: Receiving critical supplies and equipment; USS Fort Lauderdale delivery confirmed.
Airfield teams expanding traffic; C-17/C-130 and rotary-wing movements operating.
Controls, congestion and damaged routes continue to limit last-mile movement.
Government figure; independent engineering validation unavailable.

capacityGaps.capacity: 3 UN field hospitals operating; multiple EMTs in response pipeline.
capacityGaps.gap: Facility-level outpatient, referral and shelter-health gaps remain insufficiently mapped.
capacityGaps.request: PAHO technical recommendation; government RFA for TR not verified.

capacityGaps.capacity: U.S. water-purification assets inbound; UNICEF, IFRC, IMC and EU WASH support mobilized.
capacityGaps.gap: 9/10 assessed facilities reported WASH deficits; exact residual facility and shelter gaps require reassessment.
capacityGaps.request: PAHO/MOH need confirmed; organization-specific tasking not public.
capacityGaps.tr: Direct capability match; deployment status unverified.

capacityGaps.capacity: Heavy equipment, Marine engineers, airfield teams and port access now reported.
capacityGaps.gap: Building habitability, debris clearance and last-mile access remain major constraints.
capacityGaps.request: U.S. support confirmed as requested by Venezuelan government.
capacityGaps.tr: Relevant to situational awareness; not a primary TR international capability in this brief.

commandPriorities.rationale: PAHO found no referral coordination centre, restricted diagnostics and growing trauma-orthopaedic and neurosurgical waiting lists.

commandPriorities.action: Close WASH and biosafety gaps at hospitals
commandPriorities.rationale: 9 of 10 assessed facilities have WASH deficits; PPE, water/soap stations, waste bags and incinerator restoration are immediate requirements.

commandPriorities.action: Position EMT Level 1 near damaged facilities
commandPriorities.rationale: PAHO explicitly recommends EMT Level 1 support near structurally affected hospitals away from the front line. TR capability matches; deployment remains unconfirmed.
No facility information units or centralized tracking application; 9 of 10 facilities have connectivity problems.
Body bags, refrigerated transport and forensic capacity are insufficient; PAHO recommends considering international expert support.

hospitals.name: José María Vargas Hospital (IVSS)
hospitals.status: Severe non-structural damage; operations restricted to ground floor. Pediatric ED, blood bank and morgue critical.

hospitals.name: Macuto Maternity
hospitals.status: Full evacuation; zero operational beds reported.

hospitals.name: Dr. Rafael Medina Jiménez Hospital
hospitals.status: Structural damage; 35 of 108 beds operational; core services limited.

hospitals.name: Dr. Alfredo Machado Popular Clinic
hospitals.status: Structural damage; 46 operational beds; no backup generator and X-ray non-operational.

pahoSnapshot.trFit: PAHO recommendations align with TR's WHO-verified Type 1 Mobile EMT and WASH capabilities: EMT Level 1 support, water/soap stations, hygiene, hospital waste controls and support near damaged facilities. This is a capability match—not a tasking or confirmed in-country deployment.

areas.name: Catia La Mar
areas.evidence: Repeated professional and civilian search evidence

areas.name: Caraballeda
areas.evidence: Machinery and teams visible by 28 Jun

operations.title: Access
operations.detail: Port of La Guaira is operational and airport throughput is expanding with U.S. airfield support; controlled and damaged last-mile routes remain the primary constraint.

operations.title: Worksite coverage
operations.detail: Ten unnamed areas were assigned to IAE after no previous INSARAG-standard professional search was recorded publicly.

operations.title: Health system
operations.detail: Ten facilities assessed; one totally damaged, three partially damaged, with widespread WASH and connectivity failures.

operations.title: Remote sensing
operations.detail: NASA's experimental rapid product estimates 58,870 likely damaged/destroyed buildings across its assessed coverage. It remains preliminary and unvalidated.

riskAssessment.basis: Ongoing aftershocks are occurring around already compromised buildings; field reporting documents unstable structures and responder exposure.
The 29 Jun PAHO call reports 1 of 10 facilities with total damage, 3 with partial damage, limited beds and degraded referral/diagnostic capacity.
9 of 10 assessed facilities report WASH deficits; PAHO identifies biosafety breakdowns, shelter WASH needs, vaccination gaps and need for surveillance.
Collapsed forensic/morgue capacity, body-bag and refrigerated-transport shortfalls, and decomposition are documented by PAHO and Spanish field reporting.
Port access and strategic airlift improved on 29 Jun, but controlled roads, damaged infrastructure and last-mile distribution remain constraints.
UNHCR warns of unsafe housing, gender-based violence and reduced access to health, water, education and livelihoods for displaced and vulnerable groups.

lifelines.name: Safety & Security
dataPoints: More than 2,200 international rescuers and 140 dogs from 27 countries reported by OCHA
609 aftershocks reported by government
Unstable structures threaten responders and residents

lifelines.name: Food, Water, Shelter
dataPoints: 9 of 10 assessed health facilities have WASH deficits
65 provisional camps reported: 50 Greater Caracas and 15 La Guaira
Multiple WASH actors and water-purification assets mobilized; residual gaps not mapped

lifelines.name: Health & Medical
dataPoints: 10 facilities assessed: 1 total and 3 partial damage
Trauma, neurosurgical and orthopedic backlogs growing
PAHO recommends EMT Level 1 near damaged hospitals

lifelines.name: Energy
dataPoints: Government reported 90% restoration in La Guaira on 29 Jun
Power disruptions persist outside verified restoration areas
Independent engineering validation unavailable

lifelines.name: Communications
dataPoints: 9 of 10 assessed health facilities have connectivity problems
No centralized patient or missing-person tracking application
No verified network restoration percentage

lifelines.name: Transportation
dataPoints: Port of La Guaira operational on 29 Jun
Airfield assessment and military airlift expanding inbound capacity
Road controls, damage and congestion constrain last-mile access

lifelines.name: Hazardous Materials
dataPoints: No consolidated hazmat assessment located
Debris, dust and decomposing remains create exposure risks
Aftershocks continue to affect responder safety

responders.name: PAHO / WHO
responders.presence: Ten facilities assessed in Capital District and La Guaira; Health Cluster coordination
responders.activities: ERES rapid assessment, hospital-priority mapping, EMT coordination and recommendations for referral, WASH, diagnostics, fatality management and patient tracking.
responders.request: EMT-1 and specialized EMT-3 support; WASH/biosafety supplies; portable imaging; body-management and MHPSS capacity

responders.name: Venezuelan Red Cross / IFRC
responders.presence: La Guaira and Greater Caracas initial focus
responders.activities: Search and rescue, first aid, psychosocial support, family links and damage assessments; 17 tonnes of initial relief cargo.

responders.name: Team Rubicon
responders.presence: TR stated 26 Jun that it was sending a medical advance team; PAHO listed TR at stand-by on 27 Jun. Arrival and field operations remain unverified.
responders.activities: WHO lists Team Rubicon as a classified Type 1 Mobile EMT; TR also reports international WASH capability.

responders.name: UNICEF
responders.presence: Scaled national response with supplies arriving by air
responders.activities: Health, nutrition, WASH, child protection and education support targeting 650,000 people.

responders.name: World Food Programme
responders.presence: On ground in La Guaira; national logistics role
responders.activities: Food stocks, transport, storage and coordination infrastructure; 3,000 MT can support 10,000 families for two months.

responders.name: International Medical Corps
responders.presence: Rapid-response teams in Caracas and La Guaira
responders.activities: Health-facility and community assessments, medical support planning and clean-water trucking for La Guaira.

responders.name: International Rescue Committee
responders.presence: Emergency response mobilized in affected areas
responders.activities: Essential relief items and emergency medical supplies for frontline workers; additional community assessments.

responders.name: Cáritas Venezuela
responders.presence: National network of 700 sites and 30,000 volunteers
responders.activities: Community damage-and-needs collection and preparation of immediate assistance through parish-linked networks.

responders.name: World Vision
responders.activities: Food, hygiene and menstrual-dignity kits; psychosocial support, child protection and WASH.

responders.name: RET International
responders.presence: Teams and partner Fe y Alegría on ground
responders.activities: Damage assessment, child protection, psychosocial support and educational continuity.

medicalNeeds.evidence: Fractures, head injuries and burns; orthopedic and neurosurgical backlogs.
Urgent requirements include medicines, surgical supplies, oxygen, blood and fuel.
Overcrowding, chaotic triage and referral; 1 facility totally damaged and 3 partially damaged.
Forensic/morgue capacity, casualty registration and missing-person tracking are major gaps.
Maternal/newborn care, chronic disease treatment and emergency surgery must be maintained.
9/10 facilities report WASH deficits; hygiene, PPE, waste segregation and incinerator restoration are required.
No mental-health teams reported in facilities; shelters require permanent family-medicine and MHPSS support.

gaps: Current INSARAG worksite triage and completion list
Verified status of Tanaguarena, OPP 33 and ten assigned search areas
Current airport and humanitarian air-bridge capacity
Full 10-facility functionality, bed and referral map beyond the six detailed hospitals

developments.title: PAHO call update
developments.detail: 10 facilities assessed; 1 total damage, 3 partial; 9/10 report WASH and connectivity deficits.

developments.title: Strong aftershock
developments.detail: USGS M4.6; FUNVISIS M4.2 near La Guaira. No major new damage confirmed.

developments.title: Heavy machinery increasing
developments.detail: EFE reports expanded machinery and additional foreign rescuers.

developments.title: Late live rescues
developments.detail: Father and son recovered alive after four days, according to Reuters.

developments.title: Coverage gaps identified
developments.detail: Field reports identify delayed professional search in multiple areas.

validation.claim: M7.2 / M7.5 sequence and affected region
validation.note: Seismic parameters align across international technical reporting and PAHO's Spanish situation report.
29 Jun government update carried by teleSUR and broadly corroborated by AP as more than 1,700 deaths. UN reporting remained at least 1,450 earlier the same day; reconciliation pending.

validation.claim: International response established but coverage uneven
validation.note: OCHA confirms the deployed international force; Spanish field reporting documents gaps in machinery and professional coverage.

validation.claim: Electricity, water and road restoration percentages
validation.note: The 75% / 68% / 90% values originate with the Venezuelan government and are repeated by media; no independent engineering validation was found.

validation.claim: Health system overload and urgent medical needs
validation.note: PAHO facility assessments align with UNICEF reporting of hospitals beyond capacity and field medical-response reporting.
Spanish field reporting documents displaced families, water constraints and the Caracas–La Guaira access bottleneck; UN agencies report matching needs.

sourceWatch.name: PAHO / WHO 29 Jun call
sourceWatch.use: Primary source for hospital damage, operational services, WASH/connectivity deficits and medical recommendations.
sourceWatch.caveat: Internal-call slides supplied by user; figures are still evolving and assessment fieldwork occurred 25–27 Jun.

sourceWatch.name: ReliefWeb / Health Cluster SitRep 2
sourceWatch.use: Public index for the 27 Jun Health Cluster situation report; PAHO report used as the substantive source.
sourceWatch.caveat: ReliefWeb page may present bot protection in some sessions.

sourceWatch.name: Virtual OSOCC discussion
sourceWatch.use: Event discussion and coordination room existence confirmed.
sourceWatch.caveat: Detailed operational content requires authorized credentials; no inference past the gate.

sourceWatch.name: GDACS
sourceWatch.use: Authoritative international event alert and baseline technical feed.
sourceWatch.caveat: Not a substitute for field worksite or hospital status.

sourceWatch.name: USGS
sourceWatch.use: Seismic sequence validation; 10 M4+ events in the Venezuela-area query since 24 Jun, including a reviewed M4.6 near Caraballeda on 29 Jun.
sourceWatch.caveat: Seismic feed does not confirm humanitarian impacts.

sourceWatch.name: Reconectemos missing-person tool
sourceWatch.use: Civilian family-reconnection and missing-person lead intake.
sourceWatch.caveat: Do not use the public counter as an authoritative or deduplicated missing-person total.

sourceWatch.name: Conversa Corps
sourceWatch.caveat: No verified in-country earthquake operation established in current source set.

sourceWatch.name: Serve Source
sourceWatch.caveat: No verified in-country earthquake operation established in current source set.
